summary: Verified 'Void All Items Releases Table' feature - All 5 backend tests passed. When ALL items of an order are voided, the order status changes to 'cancelled', table status changes to 'free', and table.active_order_id becomes null.
passed_tests: test_01_express_void_all_items_releases_table - Express void (pending items) releases table when all items voided
test_02_partial_void_does_NOT_release_table - Partial void keeps table occupied, full void releases it
test_03_single_item_cancel_releases_table_when_last_item - Single item cancel endpoint releases table when last item
test_bulk_cancel_audit_protocol_releases_table - Bulk cancel with audit protocol releases table when all voided
test_order_with_items_added_later_then_all_voided - Order with items added later, then all voided, releases table
critical_code_review_comments: Feature correctly implemented in 3 locations in /app/backend/routers/orders.py:
3. Lines 544-566: Bulk cancel with audit protocol (cancel_multiple_items with express_void=False)
Logic: After any void operation, checks if all items have status='cancelled'. If so, marks order as 'cancelled' and releases table (status='free', active_order_id=None)

feature_verified.description: When ALL items of an open order are voided, the table should return to 'free' status

test_scenarios.express_void_all: PASS - Creates order with 2 items, express void all -> order=cancelled, table=free
test_scenarios.partial_void: PASS - Void 1 of 2 items -> table stays occupied; void remaining -> table=free
test_scenarios.single_item_cancel: PASS - Order with 1 item, cancel single item -> order=cancelled, table=free
test_scenarios.bulk_audit_cancel: PASS - Bulk cancel with reason (no express) -> order=cancelled, table=free
test_scenarios.add_items_then_void_all: PASS - Create order, add items, void all -> table=free

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - tests create and cleanup orders dynamically
context_for_next_testing_agent: Void-releases-table feature fully tested. The test file /app/backend/tests/test_void_releases_table.py can be used for regression testing. Tests use dynamically allocated free tables and clean up by voiding all items.
